matches.match_rationale: Repeat findings follow detectable patterns — a corrective action plan is filed but compliance indicators (e.g., record completeness rates, staff sign-off rates) never return to baseline or regress. Anomaly detection can flag when post-CAP metrics deviate from expected improvement trajectories, catching re-occurrence risk before the next inspection cycle. For a multi-site operator, this is especially powerful when cross-campus patterns reveal systemic vs. isolated breakdowns.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A monitoring layer that ingests compliance KPIs and CAP activity logs, flags campuses whose metrics are regressing toward pre-finding baselines, and triggers escalation alerts — intercepting re-occurrence risk 3-6 months before the next inspection.

matches.match_rationale: Each open finding can be scored for re-occurrence probability based on factors like time-since-CAP-filing, compliance record completeness trends, staff turnover, and whether similar findings recurred at peer campuses. This directly attacks the core pain mechanism: weak or temporary implementation that goes undetected until the next inspector arrives. A risk score per finding per campus surfaces which CAPs need executive intervention before they become repeat findings.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A finding recurrence risk model that produces a per-campus, per-finding risk score updated monthly, integrated into the operator's compliance dashboard, with automatic escalation workflows when scores breach thresholds.

matches.match_rationale: Inspection reports and CAPs contain structured relationships: finding → root cause → corrective action → responsible party → deadline. Extracting these as a knowledge graph across multiple inspection cycles and campuses reveals causal chains — e.g., 'student records deficiencies' consistently trace to the same upstream data entry process — enabling operators to address root causes rather than symptoms. Without this, each CAP is treated in isolation and systemic patterns remain invisible.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated pipeline that ingests all historical inspection reports and CAPs, extracts finding-cause-action triples, and builds a cross-campus knowledge graph surfacing recurring root causes and failed remediation patterns to inform system-wide corrective action.

matches.match_rationale: When a repeat finding is detected, the operator must communicate urgently with inspectors and boards — explaining what changed, why the prior CAP was insufficient, and what systemic remediation is now being implemented. AI-generated response narratives grounded in actual compliance data reduce the lag between detection and credible communication, and ensure consistency across campuses in how findings are framed to regulators.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A CAP narrative generator that, given a finding ID and campus compliance data, drafts a regulator-facing corrective action response and an internal escalation memo — reducing response preparation time from days to hours.

matches.match_rationale: The CAP lifecycle — filing, evidence collection, deadline tracking, status updates, and escalation — is a multi-step workflow that spans systems (SIS, document management, HR, scheduling) and multiple responsible parties. Weak implementation often reflects process breakdowns: reminders not sent, evidence not collected, sign-offs missed. A task automation agent can own the CAP workflow end-to-end, chasing evidence, updating status, and escalating overdue actions without relying on manual follow-through.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An autonomous CAP management agent that monitors open findings, sends targeted reminders to responsible staff, collects required evidence artifacts from source systems, and escalates stalled items to compliance leadership — replacing manual CAP tracking with an always-on workflow engine.

matches.match_rationale: Compliance officers preparing for an inspection or drafting a CAP need to know whether similar findings have occurred before, what remediation was attempted, and whether it worked. A RAG system over the historical inspection corpus answers questions like 'Has this type of finding recurred at any campus?' or 'What corrective actions were used for student records deficiencies and did they hold?' — directly preventing the amnesia that allows repeat findings to form.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A compliance knowledge assistant that lets staff query the full history of findings, CAPs, and outcomes across all campuses, generating cited answers to preparation questions and surfacing precedents for current remediation planning.
